text.fullText:
The industry needs transparency. While I understand that brokers and agents need to make a profit to operate keeping 40+% on a load is excessive and often leaves the carrier operating on a razor thin profit/loss margin. The common line from every broker I've dealt with is I can't go any higher on the rate, that's what I'm getting for the load any higher and I'll lose money. When in reality they're nowhere near breakeven most of the time. I've also frequently had them claim to be loosing money on a load.

In my opinion brokers should be required to disclose the amount they contracted to move the load for or cap the max percentage they can legally keep at 10-15%. Brokerages don't often incur fluctuating costs like fuel, tires, detention time, lost loads due to shipper/receiver delays or other issues with the load such as OS&D. Brokerage shouldn't be entitled to retain any monies paid in the form of accessorials like detention, loading/unloading.
